title: Artemisia ruthiae
intro: Artemisia ruthiae, also known as Ruth's sagebrush or Ruth's wormwood, is a perennial shrubby plant belonging to the Artemisia genus, native to China and known for its medicinal and ornamental properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to relieve digestive issues, reduce fever, and support immune function. The bioactive constituents of Artemisia ruthiae include volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into herbal soups and decoctions.
taxonomy: Artemisia ruthiae is classified within the family Asteraceae, which includes a vast array of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it among the largest and most diverse plant families. Within the Asteraceae family, Artemisia ruthiae is a member of the genus Artemisia, a group of plants commonly known as wormwoods or sagebrushes. The genus Artemisia is further divided into several species, with Artemisia ruthiae being a specific species within this group. This classification helps to identify and distinguish Artemisia ruthiae from other similar plants.
uses: Artemisia ruthiae is a plant species known for its potential medicinal properties. It is traditionally used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, headache, and digestive issues. Further studies are necessary to confirm the efficacy and safety of Artemisia ruthiae as a medicinal plant.

side_effects: Artemisia ruthiae is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine, but there is limited research on its side effects.
